# KtorMonitor

> Powerful Kotlin Multiplatform library to monitor **Ktor Client**, **OkHttp**, and **http4k** HTTP traffic in real time. Supports Android, iOS, Desktop (JVM), Wasm, and JS.

**AI Skill (step-by-step integration guide):** [`ai/skill.md`](ai/skill.md)

- **Latest version**: `1.14.4`
- **Group**: `ro.cosminmihu.ktor`
- **Maven Central**: https://search.maven.org/artifact/ro.cosminmihu.ktor/ktor-monitor-logging
- **Documentation**: https://cosminmihumdc.github.io/KtorMonitor
- **Source**: https://github.com/CosminMihuMDC/KtorMonitor

---

## Key Features

- Real-time interception of Ktor Client, OkHttp, and http4k traffic
- Full Kotlin Multiplatform support: Android, iOS, Desktop JVM, Wasm, JS
- Configurable: retention period, max content length, header sanitization, request filtering
- Security: redact sensitive headers (e.g., `Authorization`)
- Rich body viewers: JSON, XML, HTML, CSS, YAML, Markdown, Form Data, Images (JPG/PNG/SVG/GIF/WEBP), HexViewer
- SSE (Server-Sent Events) and WebSocket monitoring
- Data export to files
- Push notification summary of active requests (Android & iOS)
- Production-safe: no-op variants for release builds

---

## Dependency Setup

### Ktor Client — Kotlin Multiplatform

```kotlin
// build.gradle.kts
kotlin {
    sourceSets {
        commonMain.dependencies {
            // debug / development
            implementation("ro.cosminmihu.ktor:ktor-monitor-logging:1.14.4")
        }
    }
}
```

**Release builds** — use the no-op variant to exclude all monitoring code from production:

```kotlin
kotlin {
    sourceSets {
        commonMain.dependencies {
            implementation("ro.cosminmihu.ktor:ktor-monitor-logging-no-op:1.14.4")
        }
    }
}
```

### Ktor Client — Android only

```kotlin
// build.gradle.kts (app module)
dependencies {
    debugImplementation("ro.cosminmihu.ktor:ktor-monitor-logging:1.14.4")
    releaseImplementation("ro.cosminmihu.ktor:ktor-monitor-logging-no-op:1.14.4")
}
```

> **Note:** For `minSdk < 26`, enable [Core Library Desugaring](https://developer.android.com/studio/write/java8-support#library-desugaring).

### OkHttp — Android & JVM

```kotlin
dependencies {
    debugImplementation("ro.cosminmihu.ktor:ktor-monitor-okhttp-interceptor:1.14.4")
    releaseImplementation("ro.cosminmihu.ktor:ktor-monitor-okhttp-interceptor-no-op:1.14.4")
}
```

> **Note:** For `minSdk < 26`, enable [Core Library Desugaring](https://developer.android.com/studio/write/java8-support#library-desugaring).

### http4k — Android & JVM

```kotlin
dependencies {
    debugImplementation("ro.cosminmihu.ktor:ktor-monitor-http4k-filter:1.14.4")
    releaseImplementation("ro.cosminmihu.ktor:ktor-monitor-http4k-filter-no-op:1.14.4")
}
```

> **Note:** For `minSdk < 26`, enable [Core Library Desugaring](https://developer.android.com/studio/write/java8-support#library-desugaring).

---

## Plugin / Interceptor / Filter Installation

### Ktor Client Plugin

```kotlin
import ro.cosminmihu.ktor.monitor.KtorMonitorLogging
import ro.cosminmihu.ktor.monitor.RetentionPeriod
import ro.cosminmihu.ktor.monitor.ContentLength

val client = HttpClient {
    install(KtorMonitorLogging) {
        // Redact sensitive headers — their values are replaced with "***"
        sanitizeHeader { header -> header == "Authorization" }

        // Skip logging for specific requests
        filter { request -> !request.url.host.contains("example.com") }

        // Show a system notification with a live request summary (Android & iOS only)
        showNotification = true

        // How long to keep logged calls. Options:
        //   RetentionPeriod.OneMinute, ThirtyMinutes, OneHour, TwoHours,
        //   OneDay, OneWeek — default is OneHour
        retentionPeriod = RetentionPeriod.OneHour

        // Max bytes of the body to capture. Default = 250_000.
        // Use ContentLength.Full to capture the entire body (caution with large responses).
        maxContentLength = ContentLength.Default
    }
}
```

### OkHttp Interceptor

```kotlin
import ro.cosminmihu.ktor.monitor.KtorMonitorInterceptor
import ro.cosminmihu.ktor.monitor.RetentionPeriod
import ro.cosminmihu.ktor.monitor.ContentLength

val okHttpClient = OkHttpClient.Builder()
    .addNetworkInterceptor(
        KtorMonitorInterceptor {
            sanitizeHeader { header -> header == "Authorization" }
            filter { request -> !request.url.host.contains("example.com") }
            showNotification = true
            retentionPeriod = RetentionPeriod.OneHour
            maxContentLength = ContentLength.Default
        }
    )
    .build()
```

### http4k Filter

```kotlin
import ro.cosminmihu.ktor.monitor.KtorMonitorFilter
import ro.cosminmihu.ktor.monitor.RetentionPeriod
import ro.cosminmihu.ktor.monitor.ContentLength

val app = KtorMonitorFilter {
    sanitizeHeader { header -> header == "Authorization" }
    filter { request -> !request.uri.host.contains("example.com") }
    showNotification = true          // Android only for http4k
    retentionPeriod = RetentionPeriod.OneHour
    maxContentLength = ContentLength.Default
}.then(JavaHttpClient())
```

---

## Configuration Reference

| Property | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `sanitizeHeader` | `(String) -> Boolean` | — | Returns `true` for headers whose values should be replaced with `***` |
| `filter` | `(HttpRequestBuilder/Request) -> Boolean` | — | Returns `true` for requests to **skip** (not log) |
| `showNotification` | `Boolean` | `true` | Show a system notification (Android & iOS only; requires notification permission) |
| `retentionPeriod` | `RetentionPeriod` | `OneHour` | How long captured calls are retained in the local database |
| `maxContentLength` | `ContentLength` | `Default` (250 000 bytes) | Truncates body after this many bytes; use `ContentLength.Full` to disable truncation |

### RetentionPeriod values

```kotlin
RetentionPeriod.OneMinute
RetentionPeriod.ThirtyMinutes
RetentionPeriod.OneHour      // default
RetentionPeriod.TwoHours
RetentionPeriod.OneDay
RetentionPeriod.OneWeek
```

### ContentLength values

```kotlin
ContentLength.Default   // 250_000 bytes (default)
ContentLength.Full      // no limit — captures the entire body
```

---

## UI Integration

### Compose Multiplatform (All targets)

```kotlin
import ro.cosminmihu.ktor.monitor.KtorMonitor

@Composable
fun App() {
    KtorMonitor()
}
```

### Android

The library automatically launches a `KtorMonitor` Activity when the user taps the notification. You can also embed the Composable directly:

```kotlin
// In any Composable:
KtorMonitor()
```

Grant `android.permission.POST_NOTIFICATIONS` at runtime for notification support.

### iOS

**Kotlin side** (shared module):

```kotlin
import ro.cosminmihu.ktor.monitor.KtorMonitorViewController

fun MainViewController() = KtorMonitorViewController()
```

**Swift side** (SwiftUI):

```swift
struct KtorMonitorView: UIViewControllerRepresentable {
    func makeUIViewController(context: Context) -> UIViewController {
        MainViewControllerKt.MainViewController()
    }
    func updateUIViewController(_ uiViewController: UIViewController, context: Context) {}
}

struct ContentView: View {
    var body: some View {
        KtorMonitorView()
            .ignoresSafeArea()
    }
}
```

### Desktop — Compose Window

```kotlin
import ro.cosminmihu.ktor.monitor.KtorMonitorWindow
import ro.cosminmihu.ktor.monitor.KtorMonitorMenuItem

fun main() = application {
    var showKtorMonitor by rememberSaveable { mutableStateOf(false) }

    // Optional: system tray item
    Tray(
        icon = painterResource(Res.drawable.ic_launcher),
        menu = {
            KtorMonitorMenuItem { showKtorMonitor = true }
        }
    )

    KtorMonitorWindow(
        show = showKtorMonitor,
        onCloseRequest = { showKtorMonitor = false }
    )

    Window(onCloseRequest = ::exitApplication) {
        App()
    }
}
```

### Desktop — Swing Panel

```kotlin
import ro.cosminmihu.ktor.monitor.KtorMonitorPanel

fun main() {
    SwingUtilities.invokeLater {
        val frame = JFrame("Ktor Monitor")
        frame.add(KtorMonitorPanel, BorderLayout.CENTER)
        frame.isVisible = true
    }
}
```

### Wasm / JS (Browser)

Add webpack dependency:

```kotlin
// build.gradle.kts
kotlin {
    sourceSets {
        webMain.dependencies {
            implementation(devNpm("copy-webpack-plugin", "9.1.0"))
        }
    }
}
```

Create `webpack.config.d/sqljs.js`:

```javascript
config.resolve = {
    fallback: { fs: false, path: false, crypto: false }
};

const CopyWebpackPlugin = require('copy-webpack-plugin');
config.plugins.push(
    new CopyWebpackPlugin({
        patterns: ['../../node_modules/sql.js/dist/sql-wasm.wasm']
    })
);
```

Entry point:

```kotlin
fun main() {
    ComposeViewport(document.body!!) {
        App()
    }
}
```

---

## Production Safety

Every library module has an ABI-equivalent **no-op** counterpart. Swap to it in release builds so monitoring code — and its transitive dependencies — is **never** shipped to end users.

| Debug artifact | Release (no-op) artifact |
|---|---|
| `ktor-monitor-logging` | `ktor-monitor-logging-no-op` |
| `ktor-monitor-okhttp-interceptor` | `ktor-monitor-okhttp-interceptor-no-op` |
| `ktor-monitor-http4k-filter` | `ktor-monitor-http4k-filter-no-op` |
| `ktor-monitor-core` | `ktor-monitor-core-no-op` |

---

## Supported Platforms & Targets

| Platform | Ktor Plugin | OkHttp Interceptor | http4k Filter |
|---|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| Android (minSdk 24) | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| iOS (arm64 + sim arm64) | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Desktop JVM | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Wasm/JS (browser) | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ |
| JS (browser) | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ |

---

## Common Patterns & Tips

### Exclude internal/analytics hosts from monitoring

```kotlin
install(KtorMonitorLogging) {
    filter { request ->
        val host = request.url.host
        host.contains("analytics.example.com") || host.contains("crashlytics.com")
    }
}
```

### Capture full body (large downloads / uploads)

```kotlin
install(KtorMonitorLogging) {
    maxContentLength = ContentLength.Full
}
```

### Extend retention to one week

```kotlin
install(KtorMonitorLogging) {
    retentionPeriod = RetentionPeriod.OneWeek
}
```

### OkHttp — important: use `addNetworkInterceptor`, not `addInterceptor`

```kotlin
// CORRECT — sees raw network traffic including redirects, caching decisions
OkHttpClient.Builder()
    .addNetworkInterceptor(KtorMonitorInterceptor { ... })
    .build()

// WRONG — misses some traffic; prefer addNetworkInterceptor
OkHttpClient.Builder()
    .addInterceptor(KtorMonitorInterceptor { ... })
    .build()
```

### Android Core Library Desugaring (minSdk < 26)

```kotlin
// build.gradle.kts (app module)
android {
    compileOptions {
        isCoreLibraryDesugaringEnabled = true
    }
}
dependencies {
    coreLibraryDesugaring("com.android.tools:desugar_jdk_libs:2.1.5")
}
```

---

## Architecture Notes (for contributors / advanced usage)

- **Storage**: SQLDelight async (`generateAsync = true`) — always call mutations from a `suspend` context or via `InternalLibraryBridge.coroutineScope().launch { }`.
- **Threading**: DB writes are fire-and-forget on the library's Koin-provided coroutine scope. Never call `runBlocking` inside an interceptor.
- **OkHttp body**: read via `Response.peekBody(maxContentLength)` — the original body stream is not consumed.
- **http4k body**: read via `Body.payload.duplicate()` — the original `ByteBuffer` is preserved.
- **Ktor hooks**: `HttpSendPipeline.Monitoring`, `HttpReceivePipeline.State`, `HttpResponsePipeline.Receive`, and a `ResponseObserver`.
- **Web**: requires `sql.js` wasm artifact copied via webpack (see Wasm/JS section above).
- **iOS**: links against system `sqlite3` (`linkerOpts("-lsqlite3")`).

---
